connectorType.description: The source from which document content was pulled, e.g. an API crawl or browser history
connectorType.title: ConnectorType

fullTextList.description: The plaintext content of the document.

datasource.description: The app or other repository type from which the document was extracted

docType.description: The datasource-specific type of the document (e.g. for Jira issues, this is the issue type such as Bug or Feature Request).

sections.description: A list of content sub-sections in the document, e.g. text blocks with different headings in a Drive doc or Confluence page.

title.description: The title of the document section (e.g. the section header).

url.description: The permalink of the document section.

fullTextList.description: The plaintext content of the document.

datasource.description: The app or other repository type from which the document was extracted

docType.description: The datasource-specific type of the document (e.g. for Jira issues, this is the issue type such as Bug or Feature Request).

ancestors.description: A list of documents that are ancestors of this document in the hierarchy of the document's datasource, for example parent folders or containers. Ancestors can be of different types and some may not be indexed. Higher level ancestors appear earlier in the list.

collections.description: A list of collections that the document belongs to.

components.description: A list of components this result is associated with. Interpretation is specific to each datasource. (e.g. for Jira issues, these are [components](https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwarecloud/organizing-work-with-components-764478279.html).)

container.description: The name of the container (higher level parent, not direct parent) of the result. Interpretation is specific to each datasource (e.g. Channels for Slack, Project for Jira). cf. parentId

containerId.description: The Glean Document ID of the container. Uniquely identifies the container.

customData.description: Custom fields specific to individual datasources

stringListValue.description: list of strings for multi-value properties

customData.title: CustomData

datasourceId.description: The user-visible datasource specific id (e.g. Salesforce case number for example, GitHub PR number).

datasourceInstance.description: The datasource instance from which the document was extracted.

documentIdHash.description: Hash of the Glean Document ID.

lastCrawledTime.description: When the document was last crawled

lastIndexedTime.description: When the document was last indexed

labels.description: A list of tags for the document. Interpretation is datasource specific.

loggingId.description: A unique identifier used to represent the document in any logging or feedback requests in place of documentId.

mentionedPeople.description: A list of people mentioned in the document.

objectType.description: The type of the result. Interpretation is specific to each datasource. (e.g. for Jira issues, this is the issue type such as Bug or Feature Request).

parentId.description: The id of the direct parent of the result. Interpretation is specific to each datasource (e.g. parent issue for Jira). cf. container

path.description: For file datasources like onedrive/github etc this has the path to the file

write.description: Describes the write permissions levels that a user has for a specific feature

scopeType.description: Describes the scope for a ReadPermission, WritePermission, or GrantPermission object
scopeType.title: ScopeType

create.description: True if user has create permission for this feature and scope

update.description: True if user has update permission for this feature and scope

delete.description: True if user has delete permission for this feature and scope

write.title: WritePermission

pins.description: A list of stars associated with this result.  "Pin" is an older name.

priority.description: The document priority. Interpretation is datasource specific.

shortcuts.description: A list of shortcuts of which destination URL is for the document.

status.description: The status or disposition of the result. Interpretation is specific to each datasource. (e.g. for Jira issues, this is the issue status such as Done, In Progress or Will Not Fix).

statusCategory.description: The status category of the result. Meant to be more general than status. Interpretation is specific to each datasource.

superContainerId.description: The Glean Document ID of the super container. Super container represents a broader abstraction that contains many containers. For example, whereas container might refer to a folder, super container would refer to a drive.

thumbnail.description: A thumbnail image representing this document.

photoId.description: Photo id if the thumbnail is from splash.

url.description: Thumbnail URL. This can be user provided image and/or from downloaded images hosted by Glean.

thumbnail.title: Thumbnail

role.description: DEPRECATED - use permissions instead. Viewer's role on the specific document.
role.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Use permissions instead

visibility.description: The level of visibility of the document as understood by our system.

x-enumDescriptions.PRIVATE: Only one person is able to see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.SPECIFIC_PEOPLE_AND_GROUPS: Only specific people and/or groups can see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.DOMAIN_LINK: Anyone in the domain with the link can see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.DOMAIN_VISIBLE: Anyone in the domain can search for the document.
x-enumDescriptions.PUBLIC_LINK: Anyone with the link can see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.PUBLIC_VISIBLE: Anyone on the internet can search for the document.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.PRIVATE: Only one person is able to see the document.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SPECIFIC_PEOPLE_AND_GROUPS: Only specific people and/or groups can see the document.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.DOMAIN_VISIBLE: Anyone in the domain can search for the document.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.PUBLIC_LINK: Anyone with the link can see the document.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.PUBLIC_VISIBLE: Anyone on the internet can search for the document.

collections.description: A list of collections that the document belongs to.

childCount.description: The number of children Collections. Separated from the actual children so we can grab the count without children.

children.description: The children Collections of this Collection.

id.description: The unique ID of the Collection.

itemCount.description: The number of items currently in the Collection. Separated from the actual items so we can grab the count without items.

pinMetadata.description: Metadata having what categories this Collection is pinned to and the eligible categories to pin to

category.description: Categories a Collection can be pinned to.
category.title: CollectionPinnableCategories

value.description: Optional. If category supports values, then the additional value for the category e.g. department name for DEPARTMENT_RESOURCE, team name/id for TEAM_RESOURCE and so on.

target.description: What targets can a Collection be pinned to.
target.title: CollectionPinnableTargets

existingPins.description: List of targets this Collection is pinned to.

target.description: What targets can a Collection be pinned to.
target.title: CollectionPinnableTargets

eligiblePins.description: List of targets this Collection can be pinned to, excluding the targets this Collection is already pinned to. We also include Collection ID already is pinned to each eligible target, which will be 0 if the target has no pinned Collection.

pinMetadata.title: CollectionPinnedMetadata

roles.description: A list of user roles for the Collection.

shortcuts.description: The names of the shortcuts (Go Links) that point to this Collection.

components.description: A list of components this result is associated with. Interpretation is specific to each datasource. (e.g. for Jira issues, these are [components](https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwarecloud/organizing-work-with-components-764478279.html).)

lastIndexedTime.description: When the document was last indexed

numComments.description: The count of comments (thread replies in the case of slack).

numReactions.description: The count of reactions on the document.

reactions.description: To be deprecated in favor of reacts. A (potentially non-exhaustive) list of reactions for the document.

x-enumDescriptions.EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.ID_EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified ID.
x-enumDescriptions.LT: The value is less than the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.GT: The value is greater than the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.NOT_EQUALS: The value is not equal to the specified value.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified value.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.ID_EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified ID.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.LT: The value is less than the specified value.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.GT: The value is greater than the specified value.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.NOT_EQUALS: The value is not equal to the specified value.

isNegated.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Use relationType instead

values.description: Within a single FacetFilter, the values are to be treated like an OR. For example, fieldName type with values [EQUALS Presentation, EQUALS Spreadsheet] means we want to show a document if it's a Presentation OR a Spreadsheet.

groupName.description: Indicates the value of a facet, if any, that the given facet is grouped under. This is only used for nested facets, for example, fieldName could be owner and groupName would be Spreadsheet if showing all owners for spreadsheets as a nested facet.

documentId.description: The document which should be a pinned result.

id.description: The opaque id of the pin.

priority.description: The document priority. Interpretation is datasource specific.

shortcuts.description: A list of shortcuts of which destination URL is for the document.

alias.description: canonical link text following go/ prefix where hyphen/underscore is removed.

roles.description: A list of user roles for the Go Link.

status.description: The status or disposition of the result. Interpretation is specific to each datasource. (e.g. for Jira issues, this is the issue status such as Done, In Progress or Will Not Fix).

statusCategory.description: The status category of the result. Meant to be more general than status. Interpretation is specific to each datasource.

superContainerId.description: The Glean Document ID of the super container. Super container represents a broader abstraction that contains many containers. For example, whereas container might refer to a folder, super container would refer to a drive.

thumbnail.description: A thumbnail image representing this document.

photoId.description: Photo id if the thumbnail is from splash.

url.description: Thumbnail URL. This can be user provided image and/or from downloaded images hosted by Glean.

thumbnail.title: Thumbnail

obfuscatedId.description: An opaque identifier that can be used to request metadata for a Person.

relatedDocuments.description: A list of documents related to this person.

state.description: The verification state for the document.

role.description: DEPRECATED - use permissions instead. Viewer's role on the specific document.
role.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Use permissions instead

visibility.description: The level of visibility of the document as understood by our system.

x-enumDescriptions.PRIVATE: Only one person is able to see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.SPECIFIC_PEOPLE_AND_GROUPS: Only specific people and/or groups can see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.DOMAIN_LINK: Anyone in the domain with the link can see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.DOMAIN_VISIBLE: Anyone in the domain can search for the document.
x-enumDescriptions.PUBLIC_LINK: Anyone with the link can see the document.
x-enumDescriptions.PUBLIC_VISIBLE: Anyone on the internet can search for the document.
